label: Pre-dawn
advice: Very early. Kangaroos and wallabies extremely active — drive with extreme caution. Fog possible in valleys and low-lying areas.

label: Early morning
advice: Dawn — wildlife still active near roads. Good time to fuel up and grab coffee. Frost on bridges in southern AU winter. Quiet on the roads — enjoy it.

label: Morning
advice: Prime driving hours. Good visibility, comfortable temperatures, light traffic outside cities.

label: Midday
advice: Lunch time. Good moment for a proper break — stretch, eat, hydrate, check fuel. In summer/outback, midday heat is most extreme — shade and water are essential.

label: Afternoon
advice: Afternoon. If on the road since morning, driver fatigue is real. Consider a break. Afternoon storms developing in QLD/NT in summer. Watch the western sky.

label: Late afternoon
advice: Golden hour approaching. Wildlife becoming active near roads. If camping or stopping, secure a site or accommodation before dark. Afternoon storms in QLD/NT summer: move quickly if a cell is building.

label: Dusk
advice: DANGER ZONE: Kangaroos and livestock most active at dusk. Reduce speed on rural roads. Look 2–3 seconds ahead, not just at the road immediately in front. If you haven’t found accommodation, prioritise it now.

label: Evening
advice: Night driving in rural/outback Australia is high risk. Animals are invisible until headlights catch their eyes — by then it’s too late at highway speed. Consider stopping if you haven’t already.

label: Night
advice: DANGEROUS to drive on rural roads at night. Wildlife, fatigue, and road trains all compounded by darkness. Stop if at all possible. If continuing, drive at 80km/h max on rural roads, never at highway speed.
